title: Frontend: Authentication error handling and user feedback
description:
Implement comprehensive error handling and user feedback for authentication flows.

Requirements:
- Handle all authentication-related errors gracefully
- Provide clear, user-friendly error messages
- Implement toast/notification system for feedback
- Add loading states for all auth operations
- Implement retry logic for network errors

Error Types to Handle:

Registration Errors (400):
- validation_error: 'Password must be at least 8 characters'
- validation_error: 'First user must have admin role'
- user_exists (409): 'This email is already registered'

Login Errors (401):
- invalid_credentials: 'Invalid email or password'
- account_disabled: 'Your account has been disabled. Contact admin.'

General Errors:
- Network errors: 'Unable to connect. Check your connection.'
- 500 errors: 'Server error. Please try again later.'
- Timeout: 'Request timed out. Please try again.'

User Feedback:
- Success notifications (green): 'Login successful', 'Registration complete'
- Error notifications (red): API error messages
- Warning notifications (yellow): 'Session expiring soon'
- Info notifications (blue): 'Redirecting to login...'

Loading States:
- Button spinners during API calls
- Disable forms during submission
- Show skeleton loaders where appropriate
- Full-page loader for initial auth check

Retry Logic:
- Network errors: show retry button
- 500 errors: show retry button
- Max 3 automatic retries with exponential backoff
- Manual retry always available

Components to Create:
- ErrorBoundary for auth pages
- NotificationSystem/Toast component
- LoadingButton component
- RetryButton component

Acceptance criteria:
- All error types handled with specific messages
- User never sees raw API error codes
- Loading states shown for all async operations
- Retry functionality works correctly
- Notifications auto-dismiss after 5 seconds
- Multiple notifications stack correctly
- Error boundary catches React errors in auth flows

title: Frontend: First admin registration page
description:
Implement the registration page for the first admin user (no authentication required).

Requirements:
- Form with fields: email, password, confirm password, name, surname, organization
- Email validation (valid email format)
- Password validation (min 8 characters, show strength indicator)
- Password confirmation match check
- Role is hardcoded to 'admin' (first user must be admin)
- Organization is optional
- Submit calls POST /api/v1/auth/register

API Endpoint:
POST /api/v1/auth/register
Request: {
  "userId": "email@example.com",
  "password": "password123",
  "name": "First",
  "surname": "Last",
  "organization": "Org" (optional),
  "role": "admin"
}
Response 201: { "message": "...", "userId": "...", "role": "admin" }
Error 400: { "error": "validation_error", "message": "..." }
Error 409: { "error": "user_exists", "message": "..." }

UI Components:
- Email input with validation
- Password input with strength indicator
- Confirm password input
- Name, surname inputs
- Organization input (optional)
- Submit button (disabled until form valid)
- Error message display
- Success redirect to login

Validation:
- Email: valid email format
- Password: min 8 chars, show strength (weak/medium/strong)
- Passwords match
- Name and surname required

Acceptance criteria:
- Form validates all inputs client-side
- Shows validation errors inline
- Calls API only with valid data
- Handles API errors and displays user-friendly messages
- On success: redirect to /login with success message
- Password is never stored or logged

title: Frontend: Role-based UI components
description:
Implement components and utilities for role-based UI rendering.

Requirements:
- Create components to show/hide UI based on user role
- Disable admin-only actions for regular users
- Show role badge in user profile/header
- Implement permission checks for UI elements

Components to Create:
1. AdminOnly component - wraps admin-only UI elements
2. usePermissions hook - returns permission check functions
3. RoleDisplay component - shows user role badge

AdminOnly Component Usage:
<AdminOnly>
  <button>Delete Target</button>
</AdminOnly>

usePermissions Hook Usage:
const { canCreate, canEdit, canDelete } = usePermissions();
<button disabled={!canCreate}>Create</button>

Features Requiring Role Check:
Admin Only:
- Create/Edit/Delete targets (POST/PUT/DELETE /operator/targets)
- Create provider config (POST /provider-config)
- Update provider config (POST /provider-config/{uuid})
- Update provider status (PATCH /providers/{name})

User + Admin:
- View all resources (GET operations)
- Run scenarios
- View clusters and nodes

UI Behavior:
- Admin-only buttons: hidden or disabled for regular users
- Admin badge shown in navbar/profile for admins
- User badge shown for regular users
- Tooltips explain why actions are disabled

Acceptance criteria:
- AdminOnly component correctly hides content for non-admins
- Permission checks work consistently across app
- Role badge displays correctly
- Disabled buttons show helpful tooltips
- No console errors from permission checks
- Role changes reflected immediately in UI

title: Frontend: Authentication E2E tests
description:
Implement end-to-end tests for all authentication flows.

Requirements:
- Test complete registration and login flows
- Test protected route access
- Test role-based UI rendering
- Test session expiration handling
- Test error scenarios

Test Scenarios:

1. First Admin Registration Flow:
   - Check /is-registered returns false
   - Fill registration form
   - Submit and verify redirect to login
   - Verify user can login with credentials

2. Login Flow:
   - Enter valid credentials
   - Verify token stored in sessionStorage
   - Verify redirect to dashboard
   - Verify user info displayed

3. Protected Routes:
   - Try accessing /dashboard without auth
   - Verify redirect to /login
   - Login and verify redirect back to /dashboard

4. Admin-Only Features:
   - Login as regular user
   - Verify admin buttons hidden/disabled
   - Login as admin
   - Verify admin buttons visible

5. Session Expiration:
   - Mock expired token
   - Make API request
   - Verify redirect to login with expired message

6. Logout:
   - Click logout
   - Verify sessionStorage cleared
   - Verify redirect to login
   - Verify cannot access protected routes

7. Error Handling:
   - Submit login with wrong password
   - Verify error message shown
   - Submit registration with existing email
   - Verify conflict error shown

Testing Tools:
- Cypress/Playwright for E2E tests
- Mock API responses for predictable testing
- Test data fixtures for users

Test Coverage:
- Happy paths (successful flows)
- Error paths (validation, API errors)
- Edge cases (expired tokens, network errors)
- Role-based access control

Acceptance criteria:
- All authentication flows covered by E2E tests
- Tests run in CI/CD pipeline
- Tests use mock API (not real backend)
- Test data cleaned up after each test
- Tests are deterministic and reliable
- All tests pass consistently

title: Frontend: Setup authentication infrastructure
description:
Setup the authentication infrastructure for the krkn-operator-console frontend.

Requirements:
- Create authentication context/service for managing auth state
- Implement JWT token storage (use sessionStorage for security)
- Setup token management utilities (get, set, clear token)
- Create authentication state management (logged in/out, user info, role)
- Setup API client with automatic JWT header injection
- Implement automatic 401 handling (redirect to login on token expiration)

Files to create:
- src/context/AuthContext.tsx (or similar based on framework)
- src/services/auth.service.ts
- src/utils/api-client.ts
- src/types/auth.types.ts

Reference:
- See docs/api-authentication-spec.md for API details
- Token format: 'Bearer <token>' in Authorization header
- Token duration: 24 hours

Acceptance criteria:
- Auth context provides: login, logout, isAuthenticated, userRole, userName
- API client automatically adds Authorization header to all requests
- 401 responses automatically trigger logout and redirect to login
- Token and user info persisted in sessionStorage

title: Frontend: Admin registration check page
description:
Implement the initial page that checks if an admin is registered and routes accordingly.

Requirements:
- On app load, call GET /api/v1/auth/is-registered
- If registered === false: redirect to first admin registration page
- If registered === true: redirect to login page
- Show loading state while checking
- Handle network errors gracefully (retry button)

API Endpoint:
GET /api/v1/auth/is-registered
Response: { "registered": boolean }

UI Components:
- Loading spinner/skeleton
- Error message with retry button (if API call fails)

Routing:
- Not registered -> /register/first-admin
- Registered -> /login

Acceptance criteria:
- Calls API on mount
- Shows loading state during API call
- Correctly routes based on registration status
- Handles errors with user-friendly message
- No authentication required for this check

title: Frontend: Protected routes and navigation guards
description:
Implement route protection and authentication guards for the application.

Requirements:
- Create ProtectedRoute component that checks authentication
- Redirect to /login if not authenticated
- Store intended destination and redirect after login
- Implement route-based role checking for admin-only pages
- Add navigation guards to prevent unauthorized access

Route Types:
1. Public routes: /login, /register/first-admin, /check-registration
2. Protected routes (user + admin): /dashboard, /clusters, /scenarios, etc.
3. Admin-only routes: /admin/users, /admin/settings, /operator/targets (create/edit)

Implementation:
- ProtectedRoute component wraps protected routes
- Checks auth context for authentication status
- Checks user role for admin-only routes
- Redirects to /login with return URL if not authenticated
- Shows 403 page if authenticated but insufficient permissions

Components to Create:
- ProtectedRoute.tsx
- AdminRoute.tsx (extends ProtectedRoute with role check)
- UnauthorizedPage.tsx (403 page)

Routing Structure Example:


Acceptance criteria:
- Unauthenticated users redirected to /login
- Return URL preserved and used after login
- Admin-only routes accessible only by admins
- Non-admin users see 403 on admin routes
- Auth check happens on every route change
- No flashing of protected content before redirect

title: Frontend: Login page
description:
Implement the login page for user authentication.

Requirements:
- Form with email and password fields
- Remember email checkbox (optional, store in localStorage)
- Submit calls POST /api/v1/auth/login
- On success: store token, user info, redirect to dashboard
- Handle authentication errors (invalid credentials, account disabled)
- Show session expired message if redirected from expired session

API Endpoint:
POST /api/v1/auth/login
Request: {
  "userId": "email@example.com",
  "password": "password123"
}
Response 200: {
  "token": "jwt.token.here",
  "expiresAt": "2026-02-27T10:30:00Z",
  "userId": "email@example.com",
  "role": "admin",
  "name": "First",
  "surname": "Last"
}
Error 401: { "error": "invalid_credentials", "message": "Invalid email or password" }
Error 401: { "error": "account_disabled", "message": "User account is disabled" }

On Success:
1. Store in sessionStorage:
   - jwt_token
   - user_role
   - user_name
   - user_email
2. Update auth context state
3. Redirect to /dashboard (or previous page if available)

UI Components:
- Email input
- Password input (with show/hide toggle)
- Remember me checkbox
- Login button (disabled while submitting)
- Error message display
- Link to registration page (if applicable)
- Session expired warning (if ?expired=true in URL)
- Loading state during API call

Validation:
- Email: valid format
- Password: not empty

Acceptance criteria:
- Form submits on Enter key
- Shows loading state during login
- Handles all error cases with clear messages
- Stores auth data correctly
- Updates auth context
- Redirects to dashboard on success
- Shows 'Session expired' banner if redirected from 401
- Password is never stored or logged
- Remember email works correctly

title: Frontend: Session expiration and logout
description:
Implement session expiration handling and logout functionality.

Requirements:
- Detect when JWT token expires (24 hours)
- Handle 401 responses from API (expired token)
- Show session expiration warning before expiry
- Implement logout functionality
- Clear all session data on logout

Token Expiration Handling:
- Token duration: 24 hours
- expiresAt timestamp provided in login response
- Show warning 5 minutes before expiration
- On 401 response: clear session, redirect to login
- Display 'Session expired, please login again' message

Logout Functionality:
- Logout button in navbar/user menu
- Confirmation dialog (optional)
- Clear sessionStorage (token, user data)
- Reset auth context state
- Redirect to /login

Session Expiration Warning:
- Start timer on login using expiresAt timestamp
- Show banner/modal 5 minutes before expiration
- Options: 'Continue session' (re-login) or 'Logout now'
- Auto-logout if no action taken

API Client Integration:
- Intercept all 401 responses
- Automatically trigger logout
- Redirect to /login with ?expired=true
- Preserve current URL for redirect after re-login

Components to Create:
- SessionExpirationWarning component
- LogoutButton component
- useSessionTimer hook

Acceptance criteria:
- 401 responses trigger automatic logout
- Session expiration warning shows at correct time
- Logout clears all auth data
- User redirected to login after logout
- Session expired message shown when appropriate
- Timer cancelled on manual logout
- Warning dismissible with re-login option
